title: How to Make Today's Home Feel Like the Dream
subtitle: A reading on the gap between the house you have and the house in your head, and the small moves that close it
excerpt: A ten-minute reading on the gap between the house you have and the house in your head. The dream house is rarely about square footage or paint colour. It is about ten small habits the dream-you would have in any house. This is a reading on those habits.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The dream-house-is-the-dream-habits stance is shared with slow-living writing and home-economics literature.

practice.whenToUse: Read when the gap between the house you have and the house in your head feels wide. Also good on a Sunday evening when the next week is being planned.

content.text: The dream house in your head is not really about the house. It is about a version of you who lives in a house. That version has a daily routine, a few rituals, and a way of being at home that is at ease.

Most of what makes the dream-house feel like the dream-house is portable. The habits are portable. The rituals are portable. The way of being at home is portable. They can be moved into today's house without waiting for a different square footage.
What is not portable

The square footage is not portable. The layout is not portable. The garden is not portable. Where the house sits on the road is not portable. The paint colour is portable but slow to change. The big things are real, and the wait for them is real.
Naming what is not portable is the first move. It frees you to stop trying to close that gap in your head while the actual move is on the long-term plan.
What is portable
Most of the dream-house feeling comes from small habits the dream-you would have in any house. Here are ten that travel.
Fresh flowers on the kitchen table on Sundays.
One slow morning a week with a real breakfast.
Beds made before the day starts.
Lamps on in the evening, not the big overhead light.
Music on while the dinner is being cooked.
A clean kitchen before bed, even when the day was long.
Tea in a real cup, not a mug taken to the desk.
Friends in the house once a fortnight.
A short walk before the day starts.
One quiet half-hour before bed, off the phone.
None of these are about square footage. They are about how the house is used. The dream-house feeling is built from them. The bigger house, when it comes, will hold them too.
Pick one this week
Read the list again. Pick the one that pulls at you most. Try that one for a week, in the house you actually live in.
Notice if the house feels different on day seven. It usually does. Not because the house has changed, but because the way you live in it has.
Where this reading comes from

Original to homemade.education. The dream-house-is-the-dream-habits stance is shared with slow-living writing and with home-economics writing. The list here is gathered from the houses that feel like dream-houses to the people who live in them.
